summary: Fowler addresses the common misconception that iterative development is a new or untried approach. He notes that most clients he encounters are unfamiliar with iterative development or view it skeptically as unproven. In reality, iterative development has existed for a long time under various names. He points readers to a Craig Larman and Vic Basili article in IEEE Software that documents this history and demonstrates a long track record of successful iterative projects.
key_points: Most clients either haven't heard of iterative development or consider it new and untested
Iterative development has actually been around for a long time under various names
There is documented history of successful projects using iterative approaches
The Larman and Basili IEEE Software article provides evidence of this long history
money_quotes: Most of the clients I run into have not heard of iterative development, or consider it to be a new and relatively untried phenomenon.
In contrast iterative development has been around for a long time under various names.
key_insight: Iterative development is not a new or unproven methodology but rather has a long documented history of success that most practitioners simply aren't aware of.
